title: NEWS: Alaska Legislature Passes Resolutions Urging Congress to Keep Health Care Coverage For Alaskans
summary: Juneau, AK — The Alaska Legislature passed Senate Joint Resolution 15 and House Joint Resolution 9 , which urge Alaska’s congressional delegation to oppose federal cuts to Medicaid funding and extend enhanced premium tax credits under the Affordable Care Act (ACA) beyond their 2025 year-end expirati...

title: AST Assault 3 DV: Wasilla [AK25047849]
summary: On 5/19/25 at approximately 1940 hours, Mat Su Troopers received a report of a collision near Foothills and Clarion Ave in Wasilla. Troopers responded and investigation revealed the driver of a 2001 blue Chevy S10 intentionally crashed into a 1987 Mazda pickup placing the driver of the Mazda in fear...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Governor Dunleavy Vetoes Education Funding Bill Due to Insufficient Education Policy
summary: Today Governor Mike Dunleavy vetoed the education funding bill HB 57 because it lacked sufficient education policy reform necessary to improve student outcomes. Click here to view the Governor’s veto message.

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Trapper Creek [AK25047831]
summary: On 05/19/2025 at approximately 1500 hours, AST served a warrant for failure to appear on a Crystal Calice. Crystal was transported to Mat-Su Pretrial where she is being held on a 250$ cash bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Theft II, Vehicle Theft II, Criminal Trespass I, VCOR x4 and Arrest Warrant: Wasilla [AK25047629]
incident_type: Theft II, Vehicle Theft II, Criminal Trespass I, VCOR x4 and Arrest Warrant
summary: On 5/19/2025, at approximaltey 1034 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of an ATV theft in the area of Begich Drive in Wasilla. Investigation revealed that James Oliveri, age 36 of Wasilla, stole the ATV from the property. WPD located the stolen ATV at a residence on Aspen Ave in Wasilla. At approximaltey 1545 hours, AST and WPD located James in the parking lot near AIH in Wasilla. James was arrested for Theft II, Vehicle Theft II, Criminal Trespass I and VCOR x4. James also had an outstanding arrest warrant for the original crime of Theft IV. James was transported to Matsu Pretrial where he was remanded and held without bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Theft II, Vehicle Theft II, Criminal Trespass I, VCOR x4 and Arrest Warrant

entities.address_detail: a residence on Aspen Ave in Wasilla

title: Roald Amundsen brings in first small wave of Wrangell tourists this year
summary: Roald Amundsen passengers sail in to Wrangell on May 8, 2025. Wrangell expects almost 42,000 cruise ship passengers to visit the town this year. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) It’s a drizzly and grey day here in Wrangell. But that didn’t stop this year’s first cruise ship explorers from stepping off Hurtigruten Expeditions’s Roald Amundsen anchored away from the dock on May 8. The visitors had to get in a much smaller boat to step foot in Wrangell though....

title: USDA seeks feedback from producers about 2025 crops, stocks, inventories, and values
summary: Over the next several weeks, USDA’s National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) will conduct the June Agricultural Survey. The agency will contact nearly 92,000 producers across the nation to determine crop acreage and stock levels as of June 1, 2025.

title: Petersburg assembly to consider ways to increase revenues
summary: Petersburg Borough Assembly meets at 6:00 p.m. on May 19, 2025 at the municipal building. The meeting is open to the public and will be broadcast on KFSK. (photo/KFSK) Borough finances are the main topic for tonight’s Petersburg Borough Assembly meeting. The assembly will consider several ways to ra...

title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: May 15, 2025
summary: May 15, 2025: The Arctic sea ice has experienced a larger decrease for this week of 3.76% (last week’s decrease rate was 2.76%), from the level on 05/08/2025 of 13.038 M km 2 to 12.548 M km 2 for this week. Plot Compiled by: Howard J. Diamond, PhD; Climate Science Program Manager at NOAA’s Air Resou...

title: AST Assault in the third Degree: Fairbanks [AK25047533]
summary: On 05/19/2025 at approximately 0013 hours, Alaska State Troopers and Fairbanks University Police responded to a disturbance in the area of Iniakuk Avenue, in Fairbanks. Mogan Poulsen, age 32, of Fairbanks, was arrested for assaulting two police officers. Morgan was remanded into custody at Fairbanks...

title: The Fight for Alaska’s Future Begins in the Classroom
summary: Fellow Alaskans, For far too long, we’ve been told that the answer to our education crisis is to simply spend more—more money, more bureaucracy, more time. And yet, here we are, after decades of such thinking, ranked 51st in the nation in reading and math according to the National Assessment of Educational Progress. That is not just a policy failure—it is a moral failure. And it’s exactly why I’ve been fighting every single day to change course and put our children first. I know this system intimately. Before I...
